env: container sglang_bbuf on ion-b200 (innomatrix-us-adc-smb200-0003), torch 2.11.0+cu130, nvcc 13.0, tvm_ffi 0.1.9

impl: native CUDA via SGLang jit_kernel/tvm-ffi (no torch cpp_extension, no --use_fast_math)
notes: standard: packed bf16x2 adjacent-pair + shared cos/sin reuse; ltx2: SCALAR bf16 strided loads

change: standard kernel: drop shared-mem cos/sin staging + __syncthreads; load cos/sin via vectorized fp32 (L2-cached) -> higher MLP

impl: native CUDA via SGLang jit_kernel/tvm-ffi (no torch cpp_extension, no --use_fast_math; PDL off)
change: LTX-2 block size matched to per-row work (half32 -> 128 threads, removing idle warps); raised half32-large occupancy 73.6%->86.0% and lifted small/medium shapes
correctness: bit-exact 11/11 (pair_diff=0.000e+00) vs SGLang Triton baseline + PyTorch FP32 cross-check; out-of-place; recursion-safe fallback (incl. post-symbol-swap)
benchmark: geomean 1.3834x over 11 unique signatures (idle B200 GPU5, cuda-event, warmup50 iters300): standard 1.80x (110.85->61.73us), ltx2-small/medium 1.61-1.71x, ltx2-large-half32 1.21-1.61x, ltx2-large-half64 1.00-1.05x; >=baseline on every shape
roofline: standard ~5.6TB/s ~70%; ltx2-large-half64 ~6.5TB/s ~82% (DRAM-bound 85% SOL, no-go); ltx2-large-half32 ~6.0TB/s ~75% SOL; small launch-bound
active_bounds: half64-large=DRAM-BW (ceiling); standard=compute/BW-balanced 59/62% (bit-exact fp32 limiter); half32-large=BW-leaning 75%@86%occ; small=launch/latency
status: SUPERSEDED by cuda-v4-r1/cuda-v4-r2 (same kernel; wrapper+harness hardened); export DONE (see export-intree). Do not cite this row's 'export pending' / 1.3834x as current.

tool: Nsight Compute --set SpeedOfLight/Occupancy/LaunchStats on B200 sm_100 (kernel-name filtered, warmed launch)
findings: DRAM SOL: standard 59.1%, half64-large 85.3%, half32-large 74.9%, small 2.4%. Confirms half64-large bandwidth-bound (no-go), standard compute/BW-balanced, small launch-bound.
diagnosis_routing: analyze via Codex gpt-5.5:high (concurred: promote; standard/half32 minor scheduling headroom, half64 at standalone BW ceiling)

step: in-SGLang drop-in replacement test (AC-9), in-process variant
method: runtime symbol swap of the two public SGLang names to the candidate wrapper + jit_kernel build from workspace .cuh
results: in-SGLang oracle bit-exact 11/11 (max_diff=0); smoke standard 1.80x, ltx2-large-half64 1.00x, ltx2-large-half32 1.21x; fp16 non-captured fell back; restored

change: Round-1 review fixes (kernel .cuh UNCHANGED): (AC-4) src/wrapper.py gates narrowed to EXACTLY the captured signatures (standard [1,27030,24,128]+cos/sin[27030,64]fp32; the 10 LTX-2 (B,S,inner) tuples with H=32, half in {32,64}, x contiguous, cos/sin structured-NC strides (S*H*half,half,H*half,1)) + 4 negative fallback tests (fp16, bf16 non-captured standard, non-captured LTX-2 shape, CONTIGUOUS LTX-2 cos/sin); (AC-6) benchmark.py embeds an nvidia-smi idle-gate (abort if busy) + candidate-id + source-hash/git-sha provenance
correctness: 3/3 pytest: 11 captured still bit-exact (route to CUDA), all 4 non-captured classes fall back (recursion-safe)
benchmark: 3 idle-gated runs geomean 1.4522/1.4528/1.4605x (median 1.4528x); candidate times stable, baseline Triton has run-to-run variance on launch-bound shapes; >=baseline every shape; idle_pre 0/0/0 recorded per run

step: physical in-tree SGLang csrc export + in-SGLang drop-in test (AC-9/AC-5)
method: task-owned git worktree (git worktree add --detach $REMOTE_KDA_DIR/sglang_export HEAD); .cuh physically copied to python/sglang/jit_kernel/csrc/diffusion/rotary_embedding.cuh; cache_once + load_jit relative cuda_files=['diffusion/rotary_embedding.cuh']; import sglang resolves to the worktree; worktree removed after (no shared-checkout mutation)
results: csrc_file_present=true; in-SGLang oracle bit-exact 11/11 (max_diff=0); smoke standard 1.80x, half64 1.00x, half32 1.21x; fp16 fallback; restored; worktree removed

change: Round-2 evidence hardening (kernel + wrapper UNCHANGED): (AC-4) tests/test_correctness.py fallback now spies on the wrapper's captured _original_* (asserts reached EXACTLY ONCE) + patches the CUDA loader to raise if invoked + adds test_dispatch_predicates (gate True for captured, False for each non-captured class); (AC-6) benchmark.py refactored into parent(idle-validate+CSV)/worker(CUDA timing, exits) so idle_after is collected after the CUDA process exits
correctness: pytest 4/4 on B200: 11 captured bit-exact + 4 non-captured classes each reach baseline exactly once (no CUDA fast path) + predicate true/false
benchmark: 3 parent/worker idle-gated runs geomean 1.4417/1.4505/1.4633x (median 1.4505x); idle_before AND idle_after both {util 0, mem 0, n_compute_procs 0} recorded in benchmark.csv

step: promote into shippable kda_kernels overlay + validate integrated install() path (B200)
method: python3 scripts/export_kda_kernels/export.py b200_diffusion_rotary_embedding__multi_shape -> copies src/ to kda_kernels/diffusion/rotary_embedding/_impls/b200/, generates the zero-overhead arch dispatcher (target cache keyed by (fn,device)), flips KDA_OPTIMIZED_apply_rotary_embedding/apply_ltx2_split_rotary_emb=True; KDA_COMMIT a9e33374c, KDA_DATE 2026-06-03, KDA_SPEEDUP 1.4505x
fix: src/register.py made robust to bare exec without __file__ (export's read_exports execs it) while keeping _wrapper eager for the harness
integrated_validation: kda_install_validate.py on idle B200 GPU5: install() swaps both rotary symbols (others skipped: not optimized); integrated oracle bit-exact 11/11 via the public API; non-captured falls back (diff 0); uninstall restores both. kda latency through install() == canonical (standard 62.2us vs 61.7us, large-half64 93.6us vs 92.4us, large-half32 50.7us) -> dispatcher adds NO per-call tax. Reported speedup = canonical ~1.45x (benchmark.py parent/worker). (The install-harness baseline large-shape numbers are inflated by single-process allocator pressure and are not the speedup of record.)

summary: Continuation experiment D1+D2 (src 84080c64035a..28ceeb16b72e): D2 = standard cos/sin register hoist + full-pass block sizing; D1 = ltx2 kHalf=32 two-rows-per-CTA. Paired idle-gated run vs v4_src snapshot (f4c8b844044f) on ion-b200 GPU1: standard 61.86->57.78us (1.0706x, beyond 3% band) but D1 gave ZERO movement on its target (1x24576x2048 1.0006x; Codex residency argument confirmed) and a 12% regression on launch-bound 1x126x2048 (23.33->26.45us, beyond ~8% band) from halving the grid. D1 reverted per revert-on-regression policy; D2 carried into cuda-v6.
evidence: benchmark.csv rows candidate=cuda-v5 + cuda-v5_vs_cuda-v4 (2026-06-04); docs/draft.md continuation sections; correctness 4/4 (KDA_RUN_CORRECTNESS=1, 11/11 signatures)
sources: Codex triage review 2026-06-04 (D1 re-rank + exact mapping; D2 128-thread suggestion)

summary: D2-only shipping candidate (src 317e2fab7ade): standard_rotary_kernel hoists the per-token cos/sin vectors into registers (pair-segment invariant across grid-stride passes; launcher enforces blockDim % kVecPerHead == 0) and sizes blocks to the largest full-pass divisor that also divides the 2048-thread SM budget (24 heads/128 dim -> 128 threads; measured B200 sweep {64..256}: full-pass sizes ~57.7us vs 61.9us at fixed 256). ltx2 kernel byte-identical to cuda-v4. HARD GATE PASS over 3 idle-gated paired runs vs v4 snapshot f4c8b844044f: standard 1.0709/1.0715/1.0718x (3-of-3 beyond 3% band); all 10 LTX-2 shapes within noise bands every run (large shapes 1.0000x literal); pair geomean 1.0038-1.0066x. vs current SGLang baseline (edb1b3f8f, slow ltx2 Triton): geomean 3.1965/3.1682/3.1792x -- inflated by the documented BASELINE SHIFT; honest like-for-like vs the 2026-06-01 fast-baseline environment is ~1.45x * 1.006 on ltx2 buckets + standard 110.8/57.7=1.92x.
evidence: benchmark.csv rows candidate=cuda-v6 + cuda-v6_vs_cuda-v4 (3 runs, 2026-06-04, idle_before/after true, GPU1 B200, host innomatrix-us-adc-smb200-0003); correctness 4/4 incl. bit-exact pair_diff=0 (math unchanged)

summary: NCU-motivated bounded attempt on the standard kernel (src 4dc950aba799): register double-buffer of the x vector across grid-stride passes to overlap next-load with current compute (ncu-v3 std_v6 showed long-scoreboard 20.6 cyc/warp Est-30% + DRAM 69.8%). Result: 57.73/57.74/57.76us median == cuda-v6's 57.7us (zero movement). Interpretation: at 87.6% occupancy and 11.4 waves/SM the SM already has enough warps to cover L1TEX latency; the stall signature reflects DRAM queueing, i.e. the kernel is aggregate-bandwidth-paced (~6.0 TB/s effective, ~75% of peak), not per-thread-MLP-limited. Together with the v6 instruction-diet result this closes the standard bucket: D3 (bf16-packed compute diet) is a no-go by evidence (compute SOL 47%, not the limiter). Correctness 4/4 before rejection; source reverted to cuda-v6 (317e2fab7ade).

Corrects the evidence pointers of the cuda-v5 and cuda-v7 entries: the cuda-v5 paired-run benchmark.csv rows were lost when the v6 source re-sync recreated the remote workspace (overwriting the remote CSV before pull-back); cuda-v7 was timed by the sweep harness only and never had CSV rows. Authoritative records for both REJECTED candidates: the run logs preserved in docs/draft.md ('Gate-review corrections' section). No promotion claim rests on v5/v7 numbers; cuda-v4 revalidation and cuda-v6 gate evidence are fully CSV-backed. Also corrected: like-for-like estimate ~1.46x (not 1.49x); LTX-2 path 'functionally unchanged' backed by docs/logs/v4_to_v6_rotary_cuh.diff (comment-only delta); final cuda-v6 correctness raw log at docs/logs/correctness_cuda_v6_20260604.log (4/4, pair_diff=0 on 11/11).
evidence: docs/draft.md gate-review corrections; docs/logs/correctness_cuda_v6_20260604.log; docs/logs/v4_to_v6_rotary_cuh.diff; Codex gate review 2026-06-04

summary: Codex round-0 review required four corrections, all applied: (1) benchmark.py cmdstr now records the literal --compare-src/--compare-label arguments; the AUTHORITATIVE cuda-v6 gate evidence is the three fresh idle-gated paired sessions of 2026-06-04 ~11:0x-11:17 UTC (standard 1.0703/1.0709/1.0709x, fresh pair geomeans 1.0049/1.0050/1.0018x, worst single-run -2.77% on 1x6144x4096h64 within its ~7.6% band, 24576-row shapes literal 1.0000x in all sessions) -- the earlier same-verdict set remains in the CSV as history only; (2) kda_install_validate.py gained parent/worker nvidia-smi idle gating; the replacement JSON (docs/logs/kda_install_validate_v6_20260604.json) shows idle_gated:true with installed-path standard 57.71us == direct wrapper (no host tax), oracle bit-exact 11/11, fallback exact, uninstall/restore clean; (3) the stale ~1.49x like-for-like paragraph in docs/draft.md was replaced with the corrected composition 1.4505 x 1.0039 = 1.456 -> ~1.46x; (4) the three b200 KDA metadata surfaces were unified on one commit/speedup source of truth (see kda_kernels _impls/b200 KDA_STATUS/KDA_EXPORTS + family __init__). Gate verdict unchanged: cuda-v6 PASSES.
evidence: benchmark.csv rows cuda-v6 + cuda-v6_vs_cuda-v4 dated 2026-06-04T11:0x-11:17 (cmd includes compare args); docs/logs/kda_install_validate_v6_20260604.json (idle_gated true); docs/draft.md round-1 gate-verdict paragraph

summary: Per user direction the PR-facing comparison baseline is sglang MAIN, not the container's rolled-back checkout. Task-owned worktree at origin/main 8933ec877 (contains PR #24732 fast LTX-2 Triton kernel); benchmark.py gained --sglang-path so the recorded command pins the baseline source. Correctness vs main: 4 passed, bit-exact 11/11. Three idle-gated sessions: GEOMEAN vs sglang main = 1.4660x (per-shape medians; sessions 1.4325/1.4640/1.4740x); standard 110.69->57.73us = 1.917x; LTX-2 small/medium 1.51-1.67x; 24576-h32 1.207x; half64-large parity (HBM ceiling). Supersedes the like-for-like ESTIMATE (~1.46x, now measured) and the container-baseline geomean (3.1x, context only). v4-pair legs consistent with the gate set (standard 1.0709-1.0721x); one 1-of-3 launch-bound outlier documented.
evidence: benchmark.csv rows with '--sglang-path ../sglang_main/python' in cmd (3 sessions, idle gates true); docs/draft.md 'OFFICIAL BASELINE: sglang main' section
